framing: Nurse transitioning to Health IT / Clinical Informatics

jd.title: Clinical Informatics Analyst

requirements.text: 3+ years of clinical experience in a healthcare setting
Experience with electronic health record (EHR) systems, preferably Epic
Understanding of clinical workflows and care coordination processes
Ability to analyze clinical data and generate reports using Excel or EHR reporting tools
Knowledge of healthcare data standards (HL7, FHIR, ICD-10)
BS in Nursing, Health Informatics, or related field
Experience leading clinical process improvement initiatives
Strong communication skills to bridge clinical and technical teams
Experience with quality metrics and regulatory reporting (CMS, Joint Commission)

responsibilities: Analyze clinical workflows and identify opportunities for EHR optimization
Build and maintain clinical reports and dashboards using Epic reporting tools
Serve as liaison between clinical staff and IT teams during system implementations
Support Epic module configuration and testing for ambulatory and inpatient settings
Develop training materials and conduct end-user training sessions
Monitor clinical data quality and support regulatory reporting requirements

expected_output.summary: Registered Nurse with 7 years of clinical experience transitioning to Clinical Informatics. Combines deep understanding of patient care workflows with growing technical skills in data analysis, EHR optimization, and process improvement. Led unit-level quality initiatives that reduced medication errors by 22%. Currently completing Health Informatics certificate with hands-on Epic EHR project experience.

experience.title: Clinical Nurse II — Medical-Surgical Unit
bullets: Delivered direct patient care on 36-bed medical-surgical unit using Epic EHR for documentation, ordering, and care coordination
Led unit-level quality improvement project reducing medication administration errors by 22% through workflow redesign
Served as unit Epic super-user, troubleshooting EHR issues and training 15 nursing staff on new Epic modules
Analyzed patient flow data using Excel to identify bottlenecks, reducing average discharge time by 40 minutes
Coordinated with IT team on Epic upgrade testing, providing clinical validation for order set changes

experience.title: Registered Nurse — Emergency Department
bullets: Managed patient care in high-acuity emergency department (Level II trauma center, 60K annual visits)
Documented clinical assessments and interventions in Cerner EHR system
Participated in department-wide process improvement initiatives for patient throughput

certifications: Registered Nurse (RN) — California Board of Registered Nursing
education: Health Informatics Certificate — UC Davis Extension (2024, in progress)
BSN Nursing — San Jose State University (2013-2017)

jd_requirements: clinical data analysis Excel EHR reporting
healthcare data standards HL7 FHIR ICD-10
deliberate_flaws: Health Informatics certificate still in progress
Career changer — informatics is target field, not current field

provenance.notes: Synthetic persona and JD. JD style modeled from real Greenhouse/Lever postings. Expected output and cloud_skills are AI-generated. 4/8 scorecard checks (jd_coverage, no_fabrication, metrics_preserved, no_fabricated_skills) evaluate AI vs AI reference. 4/8 checks (factual_preservation, word_count, ats_structure, action_verbs) are structurally valid regardless.
